freshness_caveat: Coal data stale as of 2025-12-26 (133 days old); Clean Dark stale as of 2025-12-31 (128 days old). Merit-order confidence for gas-to-coal fuel switch impaired; dark spread (50th percentile) may understate coal weakness if recent prices have fallen further.
risk_flags: Storage at 34.26% (12.4 pp below 5-yr seasonal average, 11th percentile) — refill pace critical for Cal+1 power risk and summer gas pricing.
GB Power at 126.88 EUR/MWh (93rd percentile, +40% month-on-month) — structural supply tightness or LNG-to-power arbitrage driving outlier; reversals asymmetric if Hormuz reopens.
Renewables at 27.07% of load (18th percentile, -32.82% daily, -41.24% weekly) — sharp drop forces thermal baseload reliance; coal supply data stale (133 days old) masks true merit-order position.
Clean Dark at 50th percentile with coal data 133 days stale — gas-to-coal switching confidence impaired; if coal has fallen further, dark spread undervalued.

base.summary: Renewables remain 18th percentile; thermal locked in for dispatch; Hormuz premium persists; storage inches up but stays tight.

downside.summary: Hormuz reopens or tensions ease; renewable forecast upgrade; storage refill accelerates; LNG arbitrage compresses and coal merit-order relief eases.

upside.summary: Hormuz corridor escalation deepens; Iran sanctions widen LNG shortage; EU forced to bid harder for spot cargoes; storage refill slows.

themes: Thermal dispatch tightness — GB and Clean Spark at multi-year highs
Geopolitical LNG premium via Hormuz closure and Iran sanctions
Storage deficit pressuring refill trajectory into summer
Renewable weakness (18th percentile) lifts thermal call
top_drivers: Renewables collapse (−32.82% daily) forces thermal merit-order reload; GB and Clean Spark extremes reflect tight dispatch margin and LNG premium embedding.
Hormuz closure (since 28 Feb) sustains geopolitical LNG risk premium; EU-Asia arb pressure keeps spot cargoes diverted, lifting NW Europe DA and TTF security buffer.
Storage deficit (34.26%, 12.4 pp below seasonal) narrows summer refill headroom; refill pace into June will set Cal+1 power floor and autumn winter curve risk.
top_takeaway: GB Power at 93rd percentile and Clean Spark at 92nd percentile signal tight thermal dispatch; Hormuz closure and Iran sanctions sustain geopolitical LNG premium despite storage 12 pp below seasonal.

watchlist.why: Closure since 28 Feb widens EU–Asia LNG arbitrage, lifting TTF security premium; reopening would reset spot LNG economics and compress NW Europe DA spreads.
At 34.26% (11th percentile), pace of injection vs. seasonal will set Cal+1 power floor and determine if summer tightness persists into autumn.
State intervention signals aging fleet maintenance burden; reduced capex raises unplanned outage risk and gas-fired back-up demand during summer maintenance window.
Draft 'energy security' waivers weaken upstream enforcement; if exploited, fugitive emissions rise but supply relief may mildly ease TTF pressure mid-term.
Prolonged Iran crude scarcity + large US SPR release (17.5M bbl since March) cap crude rally; reduces LNG-to-crude switching incentive but keeps geopolitical premium embedded in power spreads.

watchlist_dated.name: Hormuz corridor reopening or further escalation
watchlist_dated.why: Geopolitical trigger for ±15–20% TTF revaluation and LNG curve steepening reset.
